name: dmatrix_server_fast
_comment: **FAKE / DIAGNOSTIC SYSTEM** — d-Matrix server reference (`dmatrix.server.json`) with all fabric-tier bandwidths boosted to 900 GB/s (matching GB200 NVL72's NVLink/NVSwitch per-port BW). Used in `pareto_dm_vs_gb200.ipynb` §11-13 to isolate the BW contribution to the d-Matrix vs GB200 collective-cost gap. All other parameters identical to `dmatrix.server.json`: same per-chiplet device spec (32 GB LPDDR5, 256 MB SRAM, 150 TF FP16-equivalent peak / 300 TF MXINT8 (2× FP16) / 600 TF MXINT4 by linear scaling vs published 1200), same 4-tier topology (package_d2d → dmx_bridge → pcie5-intra → pcie5-inter, cumulative reach 4 → 16 → 32 → 64), same α latencies (0.115 / 0.4 / 0.6 / 0.6 μs), and no INC support anywhere. The only delta is BW. If this fake system's Pareto matches the real `dmatrix_server`, BW is not the dominant gap vs GB200; if it approaches GB200's frontier, BW is the dominant gap. Not a real product — strictly an A/B knob for the diagnostic.
